headline: What’s Next After IBM’s Collapse? Blue Chip Selloffs Are Hard to Read.
summary: It was also the last time  IBM  stock price saw a collapse larger than Tuesday‘s 26% plummet.  The historic meltdown wiped nearly $70 billion from Big Blue’s market cap and added a new element to the so-called “SaaSpocalypse” selloff that has taken more than 20% from the  Expanded Tech-Software Sector exchange-traded fund’s sector since last autumn.  Back in 1968, concerns about antitrust investigations drove IBM shares lower.
